name: aviation_security_water_allocation_stonehaven_lib_eq
premise: An internal crisis team convened after contradictory signals hit leadership from operations, legal, and external partners at once. In Aviation Security, the situation tagged internally around aviation security water allocation stonehaven lib eq has converged with local pressure in the Stonehaven corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. A reporter has partial screenshots; if the official narrative shifts later, the organization risks a false-information allegation. Legal has not signed off on data sharing between teams; analysts are inferring causality from incomplete joins. A public briefing is scheduled; if no coherent position exists, staff will improvise talking points from conflicting drafts. The organization’s past incidents make liberty feel morally loud today, while equality is the value that quietly erodes if you always choose expediency.

agents.name: Luc Ibrahim
agents.identity: I am Luc Ibrahim, the duty officer with sign-off authority for the next window in Aviation Security. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat equality as disposable collateral every time liberty spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Mei Kaur
agents.identity: I am Mei Kaur, the risk and equity liaison pushing back on silent harms in Aviation Security. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so liberty cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes equality in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will publish a constrained policy exception with named owners, expiry, and metrics—so equality does not get sacrificed by informal norms.
I will issue an emergency blanket authorization with transparent metrics afterward—so liberty does not fail for lack of a single bold move.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Silence will be interpreted as a decision by everyone outside this room. Luc Ibrahim must choose under liberty versus equality. What does Luc Ibrahim do?
